headline: E-wallets hit 18.4 billion transactions — none of the balances carry PIDM deposit insurance
context: BNM's 2025 Annual Report shows e-payments grew 25% to 18.4 billion. PIDM does not cover e-wallet balances. Online fraud losses hit a three-year high of RM2.77B, and FMOS resolved only 22% of e-wallet complaints in the consumer's favour.

cards.big: 18.4 billion e-payments in 2025 — the funds sitting inside e-wallets carry no PIDM deposit insurance at all.
cards.sub: RM2 billion in subsidies onboarded millions since 2020. Online fraud losses hit RM2.77B in 2025, a three-year high.

cards.big: PIDM covers bank deposits up to RM250,000. E-wallet balances get none — trust-account protections are untested in Malaysian insolvency court.
cards.sub: BNM's 2025 e-money policy requires trust accounts rooted in Re Kayford common law. Vcash's 2019 shutdown used manual refund forms.

cards.big: FMOS got 1,843 e-wallet complaints in 2025 — only 22% resolved in the consumer's favour, averaging 89 days against a 45-day target.
cards.sub: Of RM2.77B lost to online fraud, recovery returned just RM6.7M to victims. Credit cards offer chargebacks; e-wallets do not.

cards.big: 63% of e-wallet accounts hold under RM50 — trivial for T20, critical for B40. The Consumer Credit Act 2025 excludes e-wallet credit features.
cards.sub: eMADANI's RM1B went to STR recipients and those earning under RM100K. Women managing household micro-payments face targeted scams.

cards.big: Every coalition — PH, PN, BN — rushed e-wallet subsidies for voter wins before building protections. The incentive has not changed.
Like issuing driving licences to millions before building guardrails — then counting the pile-ups as bad luck.
cards.sub: Bank customers have deposit insurance and chargebacks. E-wallet users have an untested trust account and a dispute service that fails 78% of complaints.

cards.big: Three fixes close the gap: tiered deposit protection, capital requirements scaling with operator float, and mandatory literacy in subsidies.
